commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=197790eb1fe071a6ec8ab39183310ac512f54069)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes the resolved memory-search sync default so unconfigured agents use a 30-minute interval and adds a regression test for that default.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +38. Total +38 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source rather than a live multi-hour gateway run: interval sync is armed by the proposed default, but the interval callback calls sync without force and runSync skips memory files when dirty is false.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer review and contributor changes because the fix changes a default and currently does not cover the watcher-silent path; automation cannot supply the contributor's real-environment proof.

target.title: fix(memory-search): default periodic sync fallback
target.body:
## What changed

- Default `memorySearch.sync.intervalMinutes` to `30` when neither agent overrides nor defaults configure it.
- Preserve explicit `intervalMinutes` values from agent overrides or defaults.
- Add a regression test for the unconfigured default so the fallback does not return to `0` silently.

Fixes #40088.

## Why

Issue #40088 reports that the chokidar memory watcher can silently stop delivering events, leaving `memory_search` backed by a stale SQLite index. With the previous default of `0`, the periodic sync fallback was disabled unless users configured it manually. A 30-minute default gives the existing interval sync path a safety net when watcher events stop.

## Exact setup

Repository:

```text
git clone https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw
git checkout origin/main
git checkout -b tui-40088-memory-watcher-fallback
```

Fastino Code was launched through the normal TUI path against this worktree:

```text
OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT='{"experimental":{"xmlToolCalling":true},"toolCalling":{"mode":"xml"}}'
OPENCODE_PERMISSION='{"*":"allow"}'
OPENCODE_DB=/private/tmp/fastino-tui-40088/opencode.db
FASTINO_CLI_NAME=fastino-code
bun run --cwd /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-repos/fastino-coder/packages/opencode --conditions=browser ./src/index.ts /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-40088
```

Prompt used:

```text
Please fix openclaw/openclaw issue #40088. This is a high-priority user-facing issue, so keep the diff narrow, use the repository and issue context to figure out the right change, and run the relevant validation. Do not open a PR yet; stop when the change is ready for review.
```

## Behavior proof

Before:

```diff
-    intervalMinutes: overrides?.sync?.intervalMinutes ?? defaults?.sync?.intervalMinutes ?? 0,
```

The unconfigured sync interval disabled the periodic fallback.

After:

```diff
+    intervalMinutes: overrides?.sync?.intervalMinutes ?? defaults?.sync?.intervalMinutes ?? 30,
```

Regression test added:

```text
defaults intervalMinutes to 30 when not configured in overrides or defaults
```

## Validation

```text
$ pnpm test src/agents/memory-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.interval.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/memory-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.interval.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  33 passed (33)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  2 passed (2)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 8.58s
```

```text
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/memory-search.ts src/agents/memory-search.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 7ms on 2 files using 1 threads.
```

```text
$ git diff --check
# no output
```

## Platform tested

```text
OS: Darwin 25.5.0 arm64
Node: v26.0.0
pnpm: 11.2.2
```

## Limitations

- I did not run a multi-hour live gateway/chokidar failure reproduction.
- The validation covers the configuration behavior and the existing interval sync timer path. It proves unconfigured memory search now enables the periodic fallback by default and that the adjacent interval-sync tests still pass.
target.author: sahibzada-allahyar
